SEARCH
Package com.scanlinearcade.games.snake

Class SnakeModel

java.lang.Object
com.scanlinearcade.games.snake.SnakeModel
public class SnakeModel extends Object
Domain model for the Snake game. This class stores all game state and enforces the core Snake rules:
  • snake movement on a 2D grid
  • direction changes with a no-instant-reverse constraint
  • food spawning that never overlaps the snake
  • growth and scoring when food is eaten
  • collision detection (wall and self) and game-over state

Design contract:

  • step() is the only method that advances the game by one tick.
  • setDirection(Direction) rejects illegal 180-degree direction reversals.
  • When isGameOver() becomes true, step() performs no further updates.
  • Food is always placed on an empty grid cell (never on the snake).

API Outline (public)

 public SnakeModel(int cols, int rows)
 public void reset()
 public void setDirection(Direction newDir)
 public void step()
 public int getCols()
 public int getRows()
 public Deque<Point> getSnake()
 public Point getFood()
 public boolean isGameOver()
 public int getScore()

Internal Helpers (private)

 private boolean snakeContains(Point p)
 private void spawnFood()

  • Constructor Details

    • SnakeModel

      public SnakeModel(int cols, int rows)
      Constructs a SnakeModel with a fixed grid size and initializes a new game. 
       public SnakeModel(int cols, int rows)
      Parameters:
      cols - number of columns in the grid
      rows - number of rows in the grid

  • Method Details

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Resets the game to its initial state clears the snake body, places the snake at the center, resets score and gameOver, sets direction to RIGHT, and spawns a new food item. 
       public void reset()

    • setDirection

      public void setDirection(Direction newDir)
      Requests a direction change that will be applied on the next tick. Illegal 180-degree reversals are rejected 
       public void setDirection(Direction newDir)
      Parameters:
      newDir - requested direction (typically from keyboard input)

    • step

      public void step()
      Advances the game by one tick.

      Tick sequence:

      1. Apply pending direction
      2. Compute next head position
      3. Check wall collision and self-collision
      4. Move snake head forward
      5. If food eaten: increment score and spawn new food (growth)
      6. Else: remove tail to maintain length
       public void step()

    • getCols

      public int getCols()
